experiences.title: Data Science Intern
bullet_bank: Architected property knowledge graph as measured by support for 3 production products with 500M+ nodes and 1.5B+ edges, leveraging GraphFrames and Apache Spark on Databricks for distributed computing.
Developed GNN listing propensity model as measured by 10\% lift over baseline by training GraphSAGE with neighbor sampling using PyTorch Geometric.
Distributed GraphSAGE training jobs using TorchDistributor on Databricks to handle massive property datasets.
Designed GraphRAG chat web UI as measured by 60\% reduction in query latency via LangChain and Next.js, enabling conversational queries over the knowledge graph.
Optimized purchase prediction with 20\% lift by training decision tree classifier for 90-day agent sales.
Engineered property data ingestion pipelines processing California real estate records using PySpark to feed downstream AI research.

experiences.title: Software Developer
bullet_bank: Architected scalable PostgreSQL database schema for ZotMeet to support 5000 monthly bookings, reducing average query latency by 45\%, by elegantly leveraging Drizzle ORM.
Engineered responsive UI components and server-side rendering in NextJS, decreasing initial page load time by 30\% across 10K unique sessions.
Streamlined RESTful API performance, boosting booking throughput by 50\% under concurrent workloads, by integrating rate limiting middleware in Node.js.
Developed fullstack reservation platform allowing UCI students seamless scheduling and automated confirmation workflows.
Refactored React hooks and implemented React Query for client-side state management, significantly improving application responsiveness.

projects.name: Flywheel
bullet_bank: Architected RLHF pipeline as measured by 4-bit quantization efficiency, by embedding Statsig A/B UX experiments via custom MCP server into AI web apps, collecting click-through rates and time-on-page metrics.

Engineered VS Code code completion integration as measured by 4-bit quantization efficiency, by deploying QLora-fine-tuned large language models (LLMs) into vLLM server with Cline for AI-assisted suggestions.

Optimized LLM alignment as measured by tuning 4 hyperparameters, by applying DPO reinforcement learning with QLora low-rank adapters in PyTorch, automating MCP server experiments with monitoring dashboard.
Engineered automated preference dataset curation using Gemini to synthetically generate prompts matching paired A/B component variants.

projects.name: hhana
bullet_bank: Engineered an ETL pipeline processing 10M+ hand histories, delivering adjusted policy lookups in under 1ms by integrating Python, Polars, and DuckDB.
Designed a novel 2D bucketing system extracting 24 distinct strategy buckets derived from 6 made-hand and 4 draw strength levels.
Implemented exact preflop GTO lookup covering all 169 hand combos with precise action frequencies to eliminate estimation errors.
Integrated exploit signals via a custom softmax-adjusted policy formula, smoothly shifting GTO baseline probabilities based on population tendencies.
Developed high-throughput board texture classification and draw detection algorithms, scaling inference across ~2268 unique postflop game trees.
Combined TexasSolver's Counterfactual Regret Minimization (CFR) engine with large-scale data analytics to produce a hybrid game-theory optimal and exploitative poker bot.

projects.name: MakerSafe (Cruzhacks Best Hardware Hack, Best Use of Viam)
bullet_bank: Won Best Use of Viam and Best Hardware Hack at CruzHacks, among 500 participants, for designing a robust makerspace safety monitoring system.
Engineered MakerSafe on a Raspberry Pi 4 by integrating Viam face recognition to identify active users and YOLOv8 for continuous PPE compliance detection.
Architected a Flask REST backend that persists maker profiles and violation records into Supabase Postgres database.
Implemented one-continuous-incident deduplication state machine, logging violation snapshots to Supabase Storage and triggering hardware LEDs.
Built an admin dashboard in Vite and React that consumes pushed WebSocket events for real-time visualization of workstation safety status.
Designed a dynamically scalable schema and state machine allowing the instant addition of new camera-monitored workstations without software refactoring.

projects.name: Apprazer (IrvineHacks Best Use Of AI In Real Estate)
bullet_bank: Won Best Use of AI in Real Estate at IrvineHacks (600+ participants) by building a machine-learning loan decisioning system.
Engineered a document processing pipeline extracting structured financial data using PyTesseract and OpenCV template-based ROI scanning.
Optimized OCR extraction by targeting specific form ROIs, processing only 5\% of document pixels while achieving 99\% extraction accuracy.
Trained a Random Forest classifier on 5M+ HMDA records to predict loan approvals, achieving 84\% testing accuracy via hyperparameter tuning.
Executed comprehensive data scrubbing pipelines utilizing random under-sampling to mitigate severe label imbalance in 11M raw applicant records.
Built an interactive borrower and underwriter dashboard using Next.js and Tailwind CSS mapped to a Flask REST API inference backend.

projects.name: Sonder (IrvineHacks Best Blockchain Hack)
bullet_bank: Won Best Blockchain Project among 500 participants at IrvineHacks for developing an identity-preserving anonymous social network.
Engineered a decentralized authentication system integrating Web3.js and MetaMask for secure wallet-based zero-knowledge logins.
Designed responsive, decentralized application interfaces and interactive client-side routing using Next.js.

projects.name: Agent Native
bullet_bank: Engineered 10x reduction in LLM prompt size by integrating CI/CD Babel AST parsing and llama-3.3-70b AOM.
Achieved 100\% UI decoupling for LLM-based AI agents by replacing queries with window AOM events.
Optimized LLM token usage by 100x by deploying versioned agent-surface.json artifacts in place of raw DOM HTML.
Constructed deterministic multi-step planning loops mapping user natural language instructions to precise AOM action\_ids.
Replaced brittle CSS selector logic by intercepting React component lifecycles, guaranteeing 100\% stability for AI agents against UI refactoring.
